campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and where money was spent that month. They also discussed plans for improving transportation around Campbell, including options for buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
New multimodal transportation plan (buses, bikes, pedestrian options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public comments, the council decided to move forward by skipping the usual first reading step and planning to vote on adopting the rule at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input on a new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up the approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Ordinance will be added to consent calendar for adoption

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly focused on administrative approvals and record-keeping from previous meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from six March 2026 sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given signing authority for the construction project

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José's city council met to check on progress with programs helping kids and teens in the community, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. They also reviewed how well the city's code enforcement is working and discussed plans to improve library buildings and services for residents.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative Grant programs
Children and Youth Services Master Plan progress update
Code Enforcement performance review
Library facility improvements and customer experience enhancements

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 13, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on April 13, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear concerns directly from residents, and plan upcoming community events. Council members broke into smaller working groups to dive deeper into specific issues and discussed various committee updates and next steps.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for resident concerns
Upcoming special events in Mountain View
Subcommittee breakout sessions on specific issues
Committee reports and staff updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three commissioners about life in Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to help residents learn about and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends money and manages its budget. The council also discussed rules requiring lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—to register with the city and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget information and spending visibility
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
How to track who influences city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 13, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on April 13, 2026 focused on economic development and marketing for the city. The council is accepting public input through multiple channels, including in-person attendance, Zoom videoconference, or phone call-in options.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting
Multiple ways to participate: in-person, Zoom, or phone
Public comment deadline: 11 AM day-of via email to mayorandcouncil@santaclaraca.gov

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to update their long-term housing and community development plans for 2025-2030, review requests from individual council members for new projects, and consider changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year housing and development plan
Council members' requests for new projects and initiatives
Changes to how council vacancies are handled
Approval of previous meeting records

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing on April 9, 2026, about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is requesting permission to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for rehabilitating the historic building and making it more earthquake-safe.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone downtown development incentives
